key_insight: TANS-FO closes the gap between clinical text prescriptions and manufacturable foot-orthosis geometry by pairing a text-aligned generative model with a real-time biomechanical surrogate.
engineering_relevance: It demonstrates a closed-loop design workflow for customized orthoses that can rapidly generate lattice insoles and estimate plantar stress without running full finite element analysis.
method: Cross-attention maps clinical-text embeddings into a continuous lattice-density field for orthosis generation.
A GNN surrogate predicts plantar stress in real time as a substitute for Abaqus-based FEA.
The pipeline is built on the PicoFoot-5K anthropometric database and supports manufacturing-ready outputs within minutes.
evidence_signal: On the Male 18-40 cohort, the surrogate matched the Abaqus reference with R^2 = 0.94 and the system reported a 34.7% peak-pressure reduction versus parametric CAD, with 0.42 mm fit error.
watch_item: The comfort outcome is only a small exploratory observation (n = 12, short follow-up, no control group), so it should not be interpreted as clinical efficacy.
paper_title: A Research Prototype for Closed-Loop Generative Design of Customized Foot Orthoses via Semantic-Physics Alignment
